items.title: Top Premier League refs set for mega £75,000 World Cup payday… but only if England don’t reach the final
items.summary: PREMIER LEAGUE referees Michael Oliver and Anthony Taylor could bank £75,000 for the World Cup - but only if England do not reach the Final. Fifa will pay all 52 tournament refs an undisclosed basic salary for up to six weeks’ work after they arrived at their tournament bases in Miami last week

items.title: Winning isn't important to Serena Williams? 'Success is in her DNA!'
items.summary: Winning isn't important to 23-time Grand Slam champion Serena Williams? "Success is in her DNA!" says Gigi Salmon.

items.title: FAI tight-lipped on venue switch for Israel game
items.summary: The Football Association of Ireland [FAI] says it will "continue to meet to discuss the operational aspects" of hosting Israel in the Nations League.

items.title: Netherlands' Timber out of World Cup with injury
items.summary: Arsenal defender Jurrien Timber is ruled out of the Netherlands' World Cup campaign with a groin injury and replaced by Lutsharel Geertruida.

items.title: Arsenal star ruled out of World Cup in major injury blow just days before opening game
items.summary: ARSENAL ace Jurrien Timber has been ruled out of the World Cup through injury just days before the tournament kicks off. items.summary: Highlights from Cardiff, where England lost to Australia by five wickets in their Women's T20 World Cup warm-up match.

items.title: England suffer five-wicket defeat to Australia in T20 World Cup warm-up
items.summary: England succumbed to a five-wicket defeat to rivals Australia in their first T20 World Cup warm-up game in Cardiff.

items.title: Somali referee Artan barred from entering USA
items.summary: Somali referee Omar Artan, who was set to be the first from his country to officiate at the World Cup finals, is denied entry to the United States.

items.title: England fans warned to expect more pain as supercomputer backs European rival to win World Cup
items.summary: ENGLAND fans have been warned that the nation's wait for an international trophy may not end this summer. The Three Lions last tasted success 60 years ago - way back when Bobby Moore captained the team to victory in the World Cup in 1966. Back then, it was Geoff Hurst who was the Wembley hero...

items.title: ‘They’ll be home in a week’ – England fan blasts Scotland’s World Cup Bank Holiday after King Charles’ shock offer
items.summary: AN ENGLAND fan has slammed King Charles' offer of a Bank Holiday for Scotland during the World Cup. The Scots have qualified for the tournament across the Atlantic Ocean for the first time since 1998.
